results.label: Draft contains a root AOP entity
results.message: Create a draft AOP root entity before running OECD-style checks.

results.label: AOP root includes a title
results.message: Add a descriptive AOP title.

results.label: Title follows OECD 'MIE leading to AO' guidance
results.message: Use the form 'MIE leading to AO' or 'MIE leading to AO via distinctive KE' where possible.

results.label: AOP root includes a summary or abstract
results.message: Add a concise abstract/summary describing the pathway and major knowledge gaps.

results.label: AOP root records an adverse outcome
results.message: Set the adverse outcome on the root AOP entity.

results.label: AOP root captures applicability metadata
results.message: Add species / life stage / sex applicability metadata on the draft root.

results.label: AOP root has references
results.message: Add at least one reference supporting the AOP summary.

results.label: AOP root includes a graphical representation reference
results.message: Store a diagram or reference to a graphical AOP representation on the root entity.

results.label: AOP root includes author/contact metadata
results.message: Add corresponding author / point-of-contact metadata for review workflows.

results.label: Draft contains at least two key events
results.message: Add the key events that define the pathway before review.

results.label: Draft contains at least one key event relationship
results.message: Add the KERs connecting the draft key events.

results.label: Draft contains at least one linked stressor
results.message: Link one or more stressors where known; this is especially useful for MIE-centric review.

results.label: Key events include measurement/detection guidance
results.message: 2/2 key events include measurement guidance.

results.label: Key events include applicability metadata
results.message: 1/2 key events include applicability metadata.

results.label: Governed KE essentiality records follow the draft contract
results.message: Key-event essentiality metadata must use the governed object form {evidence_call, rationale, references?, provenance?}.

results.label: Key events include explicit essentiality status
results.message: 0/2 key events include governed essentiality metadata; explicit 'not_assessed' or 'not_reported' counts as coverage.

results.label: Key events include explicit topology roles
results.message: 2/2 key events include explicit event_role metadata.

results.label: KERs include biological plausibility text
results.message: 1/1 KERs include biological plausibility support.

results.label: KERs include empirical support
results.message: 0/1 KERs include empirical support content.

results.label: KERs include quantitative understanding
results.message: 0/1 KERs include quantitative understanding content.

results.label: Topology validation used explicit anchors instead of graph inference
results.message: Topology validation used explicit event_role anchors.

results.label: At least one MIE anchor can be identified
results.message: Add an explicit mie event_role or ensure at least one key event has zero incoming KERs and at least one outgoing KER.

results.label: At least one AO anchor can be identified
results.message: Add an explicit ao event_role or ensure at least one key event has zero outgoing KERs and at least one incoming KER.

results.label: KER graph is acyclic
results.message: Remove directed cycles from the draft KER graph before review.

results.label: At least one directed MIE-to-AO path exists
results.message: Add or repair KERs so at least one directed path connects an MIE anchor to an AO anchor.

results.label: Explicit MIE/AO anchors have consistent graph degree
results.message: Explicit anchor roles are consistent with graph degree.

results.label: All key events participate in at least one MIE-to-AO path
results.message: Every key event participates in at least one MIE-to-AO path.

results.label: Draft exposes enough polarity metadata for directional concordance checks
results.message: No MIE-to-AO KER steps exposed enough polarity metadata to assess directional concordance. Add KE directionality (for example attributes.direction_of_change) and explicit KER effect metadata (for example attributes.relationship_effect).

results.label: Assessable KER steps are directionally concordant
results.message: No directional polarity contradictions were detected across assessable KER steps.

results.label: Draft exposes enough stressor and assay evidence for KER cutoff-ordering review
results.message: CompTox access was unavailable, so supplemental assay-cutoff ordering could not be derived.

results.label: Assessable draft KERs are quantitatively concordant by assay cutoff ordering
results.message: No discordant assay-cutoff ordering conflicts were detected because no draft KERs were assessable.

relationships.plausibility: Mechanistic support remains bounded and contextual.

assay_cutoff_ordering.basis: CompTox access was unavailable, so supplemental assay-cutoff ordering could not be derived.

limitations: Draft does not currently contain any linked stressors for quantitative review.
CompTox access was unavailable, so supplemental assay-cutoff ordering could not be derived.

evidence_items.method_description: Diazinon mechanistic context for steatosis pathway review | Mechanistic pathway context intended for bounded AOP support and reviewer-guided draft evidence use. | StudyRecord: Diazinon mechanistic context for steatosis pathway review
review_caveats: Mechanistic context does not by itself establish KE essentiality or direct KER empirical support.
context_not_assay: Mechanistic context must not be reinterpreted as direct empirical assay evidence for KE or KER support.
Mechanistic context does not by itself establish KE essentiality or direct KER empirical support.
context_not_assay: Mechanistic context must not be reinterpreted as direct empirical assay evidence for KE or KER support.

bounded_use_warnings: context_not_assay: Mechanistic context must not be reinterpreted as direct empirical assay evidence for KE or KER support.
scientific_review_flags: Mechanistic context does not by itself establish KE essentiality or direct KER empirical support.

draft_import_plan.suggested_draft_title: Diazinon mechanistic context for steatosis pathway review
suggested_ke_support_actions: Map the imported Registry support manually to the relevant draft key event(s); the handoff bundle preserves supporting evidence but does not identify KE targets automatically.
Use Registry evidence as supporting mechanistic context or bounded essentiality/applicability rationale only where the draft author can justify the mapping.
suggested_ker_support_actions: Use the imported Registry bundle to support bounded plausibility or contextual rationale for relevant KER review sections, not as automatic empirical-support proof.
Keep all imported scientific-review gaps and overinterpretation warnings visible when citing Registry evidence in KER review text.
suggested_stressor_link_actions: Only create stressor links when the draft author can trace the Registry evidence to a concrete chemical/stressor identity already supported by AOP or CompTox-side evidence.
required_manual_mapping: Manual KE/KER mapping is required before Registry evidence can be attached to draft-specific review claims.
Imported AOP-context bundles preserve provenance and caveats, but they do not establish causal directionality or direct empirical sufficiency on their own.

suggested_references.label: Diazinon mechanistic context for steatosis pathway review
suggested_references.note: Registry-imported supporting evidence; preserve bounded-use caveats and do not treat as direct KE/KER proof without additional empirical support.

suggested_references.label: ProtocolContextRecord
suggested_references.note: Registry-imported supporting evidence; preserve bounded-use caveats and do not treat as direct KE/KER proof without additional empirical support.

limitations: Registry handoff bundles for AOP context preserve supporting evidence, provenance, and bounded-use caveats but do not establish KE or KER truth automatically.

0/1 KERs include quantitative understanding content.

gaps.title: Key event is missing explicit essentiality metadata
gaps.detail: Provide a governed essentiality object even when the current call is `not_assessed` or `not_reported`.

relationships.plausibility: Mechanistic support remains bounded and contextual.

gaps.title: KER is missing empirical support evidence
gaps.detail: Add empirical evidence or observations supporting this key event relationship.

gaps.title: KER is missing quantitative understanding
gaps.detail: Add response-response or other quantitative understanding details for this relationship where known.

gaps.title: Assay cutoff ordering is not assessable for this KER
gaps.detail: CompTox access was unavailable, so supplemental assay-cutoff ordering could not be derived.

recommendations: Bring every key event up to the governed review baseline with measurement guidance, applicability metadata, and explicit essentiality status.
Tighten KE assay routing by enriching key-event titles, structured gene identifiers, and measurement metadata so assay mapping is more specific and complete.
Fill the missing KER evidence fields explicitly: biological plausibility, empirical support, and quantitative understanding.
Normalize linked stressors to CAS RN or DTXSID where possible so quantitative ordering review can resolve chemicals deterministically.
Add explicit KE directionality and KER effect metadata so directional concordance can be reviewed deterministically.

gaps.detail: CompTox access was unavailable, so supplemental assay-cutoff ordering could not be derived.

recommendations: Bring every key event up to the governed review baseline with measurement guidance, applicability metadata, and explicit essentiality status.
Tighten KE assay routing by enriching key-event titles, structured gene identifiers, and measurement metadata so assay mapping is more specific and complete.
Fill the missing KER evidence fields explicitly: biological plausibility, empirical support, and quantitative understanding.
Normalize linked stressors to CAS RN or DTXSID where possible so quantitative ordering review can resolve chemicals deterministically.
Add explicit KE directionality and KER effect metadata so directional concordance can be reviewed deterministically.
limitations: Use the form 'MIE leading to AO' or 'MIE leading to AO via distinctive KE' where possible.
Add species / life stage / sex applicability metadata on the draft root.
Add at least one reference supporting the AOP summary.
Store a diagram or reference to a graphical AOP representation on the root entity.
Add corresponding author / point-of-contact metadata for review workflows.
Link one or more stressors where known; this is especially useful for MIE-centric review.
1/2 key events include applicability metadata.
0/2 key events include governed essentiality metadata; explicit 'not_assessed' or 'not_reported' counts as coverage.
0/1 KERs include empirical support content.
0/1 KERs include quantitative understanding content.

No MIE-to-AO KER steps exposed enough polarity metadata to assess directional concordance. Add KE directionality (for example attributes.direction_of_change) and explicit KER effect metadata (for example attributes.relationship_effect).
CompTox access was unavailable, so supplemental assay-cutoff ordering could not be derived.
Draft does not currently contain any linked stressors for quantitative review.
Registry handoff bundles for AOP context preserve supporting evidence, provenance, and bounded-use caveats but do not establish KE or KER truth automatically.
Imported Registry evidence must remain reviewable support and should not silently replace direct AOP-Wiki, AOP-DB, or empirical assay evidence.
Chemical trace was not included because no chemical identifier was supplied.
Assays are ranked by key-event-derived gene and phrase matches in the CompTox assay catalog; this is not a curated KE-to-assay ontology mapping.
